decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to control the game through the use of gates and various spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck includes a mixture of creatures, counterspells, and removal spells to maintain board control. The Gates provide mana fixing and additional utility, while cards like Counterspell and Journey to Nowhere offer ways to deal with threats. The deck also includes card draw and protection spells to ensure continued advantage. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through attrition.

This mono blue aggro deck focuses on playing cheap and efficient creatures like Faeries to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By using disruptive spells like Counterspell and Force Spike, the deck aims to control the game while maintaining a steady stream of card advantage with cards like Of One Mind and Ninja of the Deep Hours. With a low mana curve and a focus on tempo, this deck looks to apply pressure early and maintain it throughout the game to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on playing cheap artifacts to power out big threats quickly, using cards like Myr Enforcer and Refurbished Familiar. It also includes synergy with sacrifice effects like Makeshift Munitions and Fanatical Offering. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and burn spells like Galvanic Blast. It includes card draw spells like Thoughtcast to maintain card advantage and tools like Nihil Spellbomb to disrupt the opponent's graveyard strategies. Overall, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and finish the game quickly.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with cheap, efficient creatures and disruptive spells while maintaining card advantage through card draw and graveyard manipulation. The strategy revolves around consistently applying pressure to the opponent while controlling the game through counterspells and bounce effects. By maintaining board presence and hindering the opponent's game plan, this deck aims to quickly close out games with aggressive plays and tempo swings.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the Balustrade Spy combo with cards like Land Grant and Saruli Caretaker to quickly mill through your deck and put multiple creatures into your graveyard. This allows you to then use Dread Return to bring back a powerful creature like Lotleth Giant and win the game in a single turn. The deck also includes ramp and card draw spells to help set up the combo efficiently and consistently. It focuses on quickly assembling the combo pieces and overwhelming your opponent with a huge creature for the win.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy utilizing Elves as the primary creature type. By utilizing mana ramp cards like Llanowar Elves, Elvish Mystic, and Priest of Titania, the player can quickly get ahead on board presence and mana advantages. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help to refill the hand with additional elf creatures, while Timberwatch Elf and Nyxborn Hydra serve as powerful finishers. The deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and maintain that pressure throughout the game.

The main concept of the deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent using low-cost burn spells and aggressive creatures. By efficiently using cards like Lightning Bolt, Fiery Temper, and Fireblast, along with creatures like Guttersnipe and Kessig Flamebreather, the deck aims to finish the game quickly through direct damage. Cards like Grab the Prize and Highway Robbery provide additional resources and help maintain a steady pace of aggression. The inclusion of cards like Faithless Looting and Masked Meower ensures card advantage and consistency in the deck.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using low-cost and efficient creatures and burn spells. By utilizing cards like Goblin Bushwhacker and Burning-Tree Emissary to quickly build a board presence and cards like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Blast to burn the opponent, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. The deck also includes ways to draw additional cards and pump up creatures to increase its aggressive potential.

This deck revolves around quickly milling through your deck using Balustrade Spy to put all your creatures into your graveyard. Once your graveyard is filled, you can use Dread Return to reanimate a powerful creature like Lotleth Giant or Troll of Khazad-dûm for a big finish. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help you dig for combo pieces, while Overgrown Battlement and Saruli Caretaker provide ramp and mana fixing. The deck aims to overwhelm your opponent with a sudden burst of creatures and finish the game in a single turn.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with creatures using cards like Circle of the Land Druid and Satyr Wayfinder, then use reanimation spells like Dread Return to bring back powerful creatures like Lotleth Giant and Scrapwork Mutt. The combo is completed by using cards like Malevolent Rumble and Molten Gatekeeper to deal damage directly to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Gnaw to the Bone to gain life and Survivors' Encampment to fix mana and help with creature abilities.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. By utilizing efficient creatures like Voldaren Epicure and Burning-Tree Emissary, alongside burn spells such as Galvanic Blast and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to quickly deplete the opponent's life total. Support cards like Goblin Bushwhacker and Rally at the Hornburg help to boost the team's attacking power, while Wrenn's Resolve and Clockwork Percussionist provide protection and additional damage. The strategy revolves around fast-paced aggression and relentless pressure to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of efficient and mana-producing Elf creatures. By quickly ramping up mana with cards like Priest of Titania and Elvish Mystic, the deck aims to play powerful threats such as Nyxborn Hydra and Timberwatch Elf to dominate the board. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help to refill the hand with more Elves, while Quirion Ranger provides additional utility. Spidersilk Armor protects against aggressive opponents, while Wellwisher helps to gain life and stay in the game against aggressive decks.

This Pauper 1 vs 1 Aggro Jund deck focuses on quickly building up a board presence through sacrificing creatures and generating value. By utilizing cards like Refurbished Familiar and Writhing Chrysalis, the deck can create tokens and generate card advantage. The deck also includes various removal spells like Cast Down and Cleansing Wildfire to control the opponent's board. The inclusion of cards like Sagu Wildling and Eviscerator's Insight provide additional reach and ways to close out the game. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and removal spells.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly build a board presence with efficient and mana-producing Elves to ramp into powerful creatures like Nyxborn Hydra and Avenging Hunter. The deck utilizes cards like Lead the Stampede and Winding Way to quickly refill the hand with more creatures to maintain pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Timberwatch Elf and Masked Vandal provides additional utility and removal options to support the aggressive strategy of overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of Elves.

This deck focuses on dealing damage quickly to the opponent using low-cost spells and creatures with haste. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with burn spells and aggressive creatures early on in the game. The deck also includes cards that allow for card draw and additional damage when casting spells. By using efficient spells and creatures, the deck aims to finish off the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize or mount a strong defense.

The focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a low-cost, aggressive strategy using cheap spells and creatures. By utilizing cards like Tolarian Terror, Cryptic Serpent, and Delver of Secrets, the deck aims to control the board and deal damage efficiently. Other cards like Thought Scour and Mental Note help fill up the graveyard to reduce the cost of spells like Cryptic Serpent. Counterspells like Dispel and Spell Pierce help protect the board while maintaining pressure. Overall, the deck seeks to build a strong offense while maintaining control and tempo.
